what_it_does: Implements a file-discovered registry for board item types (story kinds). It scans configured directories for Python files, loads each module, reads a module-level ITEM_TYPE dict, validates it, and exposes the resulting ItemType objects through a dict-like ItemTypeRegistry. Each item type carries its own state lifecycle (initial state, legal states, and allowed transitions).
observations: Uses os.listdir plus importlib to discover item-type modules at runtime.
Enforces that the declared id equals the filename and that required fields are present.
Validates that initial and all transition endpoints are members of the declared states list.
Stores the original ITEM_TYPE dict verbatim in ItemType.spec while providing typed accessors.
Provides discover, rediscover, register, as_records, and dict-like lookup methods.
Skips files starting with underscore or not ending in .py during directory scanning.
Mirrors the design language of ProjectionRegistry / RoleRegistry / RelationTypeRegistry.
standouts: The id-must-equal-filename validation ties addressability directly to the filesystem.
The per-type state machine (states, initial, transitions) is treated as row data rather than hardcoded logic.
Fail-loud validation: malformed declarations raise immediately at discovery time.
conventions: Follows a registry pattern with dict-like __getitem__, __contains__, __iter__, __len__, get.
Uses a dataclass to represent a registry row.
Declares module-level constants for allowed and required fields.
Uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location for dynamic module loading.
Docstrings explain design rationale and reference project laws/principles.
concerns: _load_module executes arbitrary Python files from the scanned directories, which is unsafe if directories are untrusted.
discover silently continues when a directory does not exist, which could mask misconfiguration.
register and discover overwrite existing ids without warning, so duplicate ids across directories last-one-wins.
No thread-safety mechanisms around rediscover or mutation of the registry.
No validation that every state has at least one outgoing transition, so dead-end states are allowed.
notes: This file is the runtime loader/validator for story-type definitions; adding a new board item type is meant to be a file drop, not a code change.
questions: Where are the item_types directories located and who configures them?
Who calls discover or rediscover, and when?
How are validation errors surfaced to end users versus developers?
Are there example item_types/*.py files elsewhere in the project?
How do board items reference these types and enforce lifecycle transitions at runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a file-discovered, validated item-type registry with per-type lifecycles, and the code directly implements that purpose.
apparent_themes: One consistent registry mechanism across the project
Lifecycle encoded in the type row
intention_signals: The code is trying to make story-type definitions data-driven and externally extensible, while keeping lifecycle rules close to the type declaration and aligned with sibling registry implementations.
novelty: Distinctively treats each item type as a self-contained state-machine row discovered from the filesystem, with strict identity coupling between filename and declared id.
contribution: Provides a validated, dict-like, file-discovered registry for item types and their lifecycles, allowing new board story types to be added without modifying core code.
summary_for_embedding: A Python module that discovers and validates file-based item-type declarations, each carrying a per-type state lifecycle, and exposes them through a dict-like ItemTypeRegistry.
intention_for_embedding: To let new board story types be added by dropping a Python file that declares an ITEM_TYPE row, with lifecycle transitions validated at discovery time.

symbols.does: Dataclass representing a discovered item type with typed accessors and lifecycle transitions.
symbols.description: ItemType stores the validated ITEM_TYPE row in spec and exposes id, initial, states, transitions, plus optional label and desc accessors. It is the runtime object returned by the registry.

symbols.does: Property returning the human label, falling back to the type id.
symbols.description: Provides a readable name for the item type; if no label is declared in the spec, the id is used instead.

symbols.does: Property returning the optional operator-facing description.
symbols.description: Returns the desc field from the spec if present, otherwise None.

symbols.does: Returns the list of states reachable from a given state per the type's transitions.
symbols.description: Looks up the from-state in transitions and returns a copy of the target states list; empty if no transition is declared.

symbols.does: Loads a Python file via importlib and returns its basename and module object.
symbols.description: Creates a unique module name prefixed with _it_, executes the file, and returns the discovered module so its ITEM_TYPE attribute can be read.

symbols.does: Validates an ITEM_TYPE dict and constructs an ItemType, raising on any malformed data.
symbols.description: Checks type, id/filename equality, known fields, required fields, states list, initial membership, and transition shape/endpoint membership before returning an ItemType.

symbols.does: File-discovered container and dict-like registry for ItemType objects.
symbols.description: ItemTypeRegistry owns the collection of discovered item types and provides discovery, rediscovery, registration, record export, and standard mapping-style access.
Sets up the internal mapping that will hold validated ItemType objects keyed by id.

symbols.does: Scans directories for .py files and registers those declaring an ITEM_TYPE dict.
symbols.description: Iterates sorted directory listings, skips underscore and non-.py files, loads each module, and registers the ITEM_TYPE declaration if present; silently skips directories that do not exist.

symbols.does: Clears the registry and re-runs discovery over the given directories.
symbols.description: Used to refresh the item-type set at runtime by emptying item_types and calling discover again.

symbols.does: Validates and stores an ItemType under the given module name.
symbols.description: Calls _build_item_type and assigns the result to item_types[name], overwriting any existing entry without warning.

symbols.does: Returns a sorted list of the raw ITEM_TYPE spec dicts.
symbols.description: Exports the original declarations in a records format, ordered by item-type id.

symbols.does: Looks up an ItemType by id, raising KeyError if missing.
symbols.description: Provides direct bracket access to registered item types; unknown ids propagate the underlying KeyError.

symbols.does: Membership test for an id in the registry.
symbols.description: Returns True if the given id is registered, otherwise False.
Yields the keys of the internal item_types mapping.

symbols.does: Returns the count of registered item types.
symbols.description: Reports how many item types are currently loaded in the registry.

symbols.does: Looks up an ItemType by id with an optional default.
symbols.description: Returns the registered ItemType or the supplied default if the id is not present.
